def: adj. S. लक्ष A hundred thousand; a lac. 1. Use ek pān lākh lākh banāo detā hai . Wom. One pān makes her a lac lac times more beautiful. 2. Lākh kulfat ko dekhte haiṅ ham, Dugnā ulfat ko dekhte haiṅ ham . Though sorrows hundred thousand meet my eye Yet twice as much affection I descry. 3. Lākhoṅ meṅ ek hai . He is one in a million. lākh, kitnā hī , adv. Any number; heaps; any extent. lākh jī se , adv. With all one's heart. Koī lākh jī se ho mujh par fidā, Maiṅ tujh par fidā hūṅ, mujhe is se kyā ? Mīr Hasan. What though a lak times be a heart devote to me, What's it to me? my heart is all devote to thee. lākh sir kā h. , v. n. 1. To insist; persist. 2. To turn a deaf ear to. lākh kā ghar khāk h. , v. n. To be reduced to poverty. lākh man kā h. , v. n. 1. To be very heavy or immovable. 2. To be a man of weight or consideration. lākhoṅ meṅ , adv. 1. Before every body; in public. 2. Certainly; assuredly. Woh lākhoṅ meṅ āëgā. lakh-luṭ , adj. Extravagant; prodigal.
more: n. m. S. लाक्षा. 1. Gum lac. 2. Sealing wax. Lākh ṭake kī ser, paise kī kitnī ? A pun. lākh kā chūṛā , n. m. Bangles made of lac. Chūṛā hai lākh kā! Street cry. lākh kī battī , n. f. A stick of sealing wax. chapṛā lākh . Shell lac.

s. m. 1. The voice of a stork. 2. A continual motion of the jaw or of the tongue (in serpents). 3. Eloquence, pronunciation.

Hāth kī lakaṛyā līnī, kāṅdhe ke kamalyā līnī, sādhū ko dukh de gayo re . Sūr Dās. His staff from him he took, his blanket took, the holy man he hath afflicted sore. lakṛī , adj. 1. Stift; rigid. 2. Maiṅ tumhāre sāmne mar-jāūṅ, aur tum mujhe lakaṛiyāṅ do!
Lakshadweep (an archipelago and union territory of India in the Arabian Sea)

def: s.f. The red animal dye called 'lac'; — the insect which produces lac (it is analogous to the cochineal insect, and, like it, forms, when dried and prepared, a dye of a red colour; its nest is formed of a resinous substance which is used as sealing-wax, and is called shell-lac, vulg. shellac)
subs: s.m. The red Lodh-tree (from the bark of which an astringent infusion is prepared which is used to fix colour in dyeing)

more: n. f. Pālī, lakkhī ; Pr. lachchhī . 1. The wife of Vishnu, the goddess of wealth and prosperity. She is also called Padma, Kāmdā, and Srī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the Romans. 2. The Hin. worship of Lakshmī on the last day of the dark fortnight of Kārtik (Sept.—Oct.). 2. A ceremony in honor of the same, observed by a bride and bridegroom when the bride has been brought to the house of her husband. lakshmī ghar meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To prosper; thrive. 2. To have a run of good luck. lakshmī nārāin k. , v. a. 1. To offer one's meal to Lakshmī and Nārāin before partaking of it. 2. To begin to eat.

(wrongly written لعل) A Hin. surname. lāl aṅgārā, lāl surkh , adj. 1. Red hot. 2. Very red. 3. Flushed with rage; fiery. ety: Prk. ललओ, or लल्लओ?; S. लड or लल+कः
def: s.m. A boy; dear boy, darling; — a dolt, blockhead, fool ( = S. laṭa); — adj. (f. -ī), Impotent
more: n. m. 1. A term of endearment. 2. A boy. 3. ( lalā ) A green horn (احمق). 1. Mere lalā kī ulṭī rīt, Sāvan mās uṭhāveṅ bhīt . Prov. My son must needs the practice of the world amend, My simple builds his wall when Sāvan rains descend. 2. Ghuṅgṭā! terī oṭ karan nā pāī do batiyāṅ, Lalnā ko maiṅ palnā meṅ ḍāl āī, chalī āī tere sāth . Rus. Wom. Song. O veil that wraps my face! through thee two words I could not say, My babe I've in his cradle laid and come with you away. 3. Chhallā de to lallā pāve, Paisā de to god khilāve . Hījrās. Eunuchs. A ring give me, a son shall be your meed, Pice give, a child you shall in your lap feed! badāūṅ ke lalā , n. m. An arrant fool of Badāun.
subs: A Badāʼuṅ fool; a great dolt or blockhead (cf. the Eng. 'Essex calf').

def: s.m. Longing, eager desire; covetousness, cupidity, greed, avarice; selfishness; occasion; view; want; — incentive to cupidity, a bait, lure
more: n. m. S. लालसा 1. Covetousness (طمع 1). 1. Lālach gun ghar binās . Prov. Greed destroys every good quality. 2. Lālach bas partok nasāë . Prov. In toils of greed Heaven lost. 3. Makkhī baiṭhī shahad par aur pankh gaë lipṭāë, Hāth male aur sir dhune, lālach burī balāë . Dohā. A fly upon the honey lights and in 't his wings are stuck, He rubs his hands and beats his head, greed brings such evil luck! lālach denā , v. a. To allure; entice; decoy. lālach k. , v. a. To be greedy, avaricious. lālach meṅ ānā , v. n. To take the bait.
s. m. Longing, covetousness, greediness, selfishness, avarice, occasion, view, want. لالچ کرنا lālach karnā, To covet, to long for.

r: lām, l., la, antah-stha, abjad, Shawwāl., laghu., l, r, ṛ
def: the thirtieth letter of the Urdū or Hindūstānī alphabet (the twenty-seventh of the Persian alphabet, and the twenty-third of the Arabic), has the sound of the English l. The corresponding Hindī letter is ल la, the twenty-eighth consonant of the Devanāgarī alphabet, and the third of the semi-vowels or antah-stha letters. In reckoning by abjad, lām stands for 30; it is also used as an abbreviation for the month Shawwāl. In works on Hindī Prosody, ल la is used as an abbreviation of the word लघु laghu. The letter l is commonly interchanged with r and ṛ, as will have been seen in what has preceded, and as will be pointed out in what follows.
more: the 23rd letter of the Arabic, and 27th of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ल in Sanskrit and Hindī. It is one of the eight dental letters in Arabic, and its sound is that of the English l . In abjad it stands for 30, and, as an abbreviation for the month Shavvāl . As an initial, medial, and final it is interchangeable with t, r, s, k , q. v.; with t , as lag=tak till; sulānā =sutānā put to sleep; rāval=rāvat king; pīlā=pīt , pale: with ṭ , as laiyā=ṭaiyā shell; luppo=ṭuppo cap; kapāl=kapāṭ skull: with d , q. v. as lahaknā=dahaknā to blaze; W. bulbulā = E. budbudā a bubble; kurel=kured scrape; Panj. chugal = P. chugad owl: with n , as E. baṅgā = W. naṅgā naked; lon=non salt'; līl=nīl indigo; phulaṅg=phunaṅg the end of a branch; jalam=janam birth; mallū=manṅū monkey; A. sandal = H. chandan sandal; suhāgal= suhāgan a feme covert . As a medial and final with ṛ , as haldī = Rus. hardī tu…

the twenty-third letter of the Arabic, and twenty-seventh of the Persian alphabet, corresponds to ल in Sanskrit. It is one of the eight dental letters in Arabic, and its sound is that of the English l. In the denotation of numbers by the letters of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for 30: and, by way of abbreviation, it may represent the month شوال. Its permutations are, 1. In Persian and Hindūstānī into ر and vice versa; as, زرو zarū for زلو zalū, A leech; بار for بال Hair; ديوال for ديوار A wall; تلوار for تروار A sword. 2. In Arabic, in the article, into م; as, ليس من امبرمصيام في امسفر laisa mina-m-birri-m-ṣiyāmu fi-m-safari, for ليس من البرالصيام في السفر laisa mina-l-birri-ṣ-ṣiyāmu fi-s-safari, It is not a virtuous act to fast on a journey. In Arabic it may be prefixed to a word, for various purposes: as, 1. Pronounced with the vowel fatʼḥa, and prefixed to the aorist of a verb, for …

ety: contrac. of le-ānā, lit. 'having taken to come, or to take and come'
def: v.t. To bring, fetch; to bring forward, to adduce; to bring in, introduce, usher in; to import; to bring under, to include (in); to bring (a person) to agree (to, -par), to induce, persuade, win over (to); to apply; to purchase, buy; to bring forth, beget, breed, yield, produce, make (N.B. — lānā, although it governs the acc. case, is, since a neut. verb enters into its construction, treated as a neuter verb in the tenses formed by means of the perfect part.; the nom. case, and not the agent being employed; e.g. maiṅ usko lāyā hūṅ, 'I have brought him or it,' not maiṅ-ne usko lāyā hai)
more: v. n. S. ला bring. 1. To bring; fetch. 'Tum baṛe sajan kī dhī, jabānī mat karo, jī! Tum par lāūṅgā saut, saukan lāūṅ biyāh-ke, jī! ' Play. ' Ek nahīṅ, do chār, ḍarāī nāëṅ ḍarūṅ, jī mahārāj! ' 'A great man's daughter you forsooth! cease bandying words with me! A co-wife I'll bring home, a wedded co-wife she shall be.' 'Not one, bring three or four, dont think, my Lord, you'll frighten me!' 2. To bring forward; carry over. 3. To introduce; usher in. 4. To lead; draw or bring over; win over. 5. ( andar lānā ) To import. 6. ( dar lānā ) To bring under; include; comprehend. 7. To produce; yield; breed; beget. 1. Raṅg lānā . 2. Fail lānā . 8. To pimp. 9. To purchase; buy. Jis bhāo lānā usī bhāo bechnā . We sell as we buy. gair mulk se gulām ke taur par lānā . G. G. To import as a slave.
v. n. 1. To bring, adduce, induce, introduce, persuade, apply. 2. To breed, produce, make.
subs: s.f. Assessment of land according to the number of ploughs employed on it
s.m. Taking cattle in lieu of money (from a debtor).
wety: Two theories, or conflation of both: * A reduction from the light verb construction لے آنا (le ānā) with the same meaning, explaining the lack of an ergative marker for its subject as آنا (ānā) is intransitive. Compare Punjabi ਲਿਆਉਣਾ (liāuṇā) which more transparently is a lexicalized light verb construction, preserving the original vowels. Nepali ल्याउनु (lyāunu), Gujarati લાવવું (lāvvũ) may also reflect the same.…
